data.reasoning: Both models skipped the critical PKGBUILD file after misclassifying it as binary, preventing any meaningful security assessment. Without reviewing the PKGBUILD (which defines build/install behavior), no safety determination can be made.
coverage_issues: PKGBUILD was skipped by both models despite being the core build script
No files were reviewed due to critical failure in file type detection
false_negatives: All potential risks in PKGBUILD were missed due to skipped review
Possible .install scripts or build hooks were not examined
learnings: Critical flaw in file detection: Tool must never skip PKGBUILD. Implement fallback decoding for misclassified text files
Prioritization failure: Audit must abort if PKGBUILD can't be read rather than proceeding with zero coverage
Improve binary detection heuristics: PKGBUILDs are always text - special-case known critical files
Add validation step: Confirm at least PKGBUILD was successfully read before proceeding
